title: Loud Eaters and Phones Ruin Cinema Trips
summary: Going to the movies can be fun, but sometimes other people ruin it. They talk loudly, eat noisily, or use their phones during the film. The writer had a bad experience watching a movie called Project Hail Mary. Someone was translating the movie for another person, and others were on their phones. A family even had a whole picnic! At another movie, a woman ate a big chocolate heart with a crinkly wrapper. People try to stop the noise by saying 'shh' or talking to the offenders, but it still distracts everyone. A TikToker named Cerys Hawkes says going to early shows or smaller cinemas can help avoid disruptions. She once saw two men filming themselves with flash on during a scary movie. They only stopped when staff came. Some people even throw popcorn or bring chickens to the cinema! It's important to be quiet so everyone can enjoy the film.

background_read: Cinemas are places where people watch movies together. It is important to be quiet so everyone can hear the movie. Using phones or talking loudly can disturb others. Some cinemas show ads asking people to be quiet.
Article_Structure: WHO: The writer and other audience members who were disturbed by noisy people.
WHAT: People talking loudly, eating noisily, and using phones during movies.
WHERE: At a cinema in London and other cinemas.
WHY: Because some people forget to be quiet and disturb others.

perspectives.description: They want to enjoy the movie without distractions.
They might not realize they are being annoying or they don't care.
